name: Galaktoboureko - Creamy Custard Pastry
servings: prep. time 45 minutes | cooking time 1 hour | serves 12-15
method: In a bowl, combine the thickener ingredients. Sift or use a whisk or wooden spoon to break up any lumps. Set aside.
In a saucepan, bring the milk to a low boil over medium heat.
Gradually add the thickener ingredients to the boiling milk, stirring continuously.
Continue stirring with a wooden spoon until thickened and bubbling.
Remove from heat and set aside.
Using an electric mixer, beat the eggs at high speed.
Add the ½ cup of sugar and continue mixing until the mixture turns pale yellow (about 10 minutes)
Fold the egg mixture into the hot milk mixture to make a custard. Cover partially and set it aside to cool.
Butter a 9x13 baking dish, and layer 7 sheets of phyllo into the pan, brushing each one with butter as you lay it in.
Pour the custard into the pan over the phyllo, and cover with the remaining 5 sheets of phyllo, brushing each sheet with butter as you lay it down.
Bake until the top crust is crisp and the custard filling has set (40 to 45 minutes).
To make a syrup, stir together 1 cup sugar and 1 cup of water in a small saucepan. Bring it to a boil.
After taking the Galaktoboureko out of the oven, spoon the hot sugar syrup over the top, particularly the edges.
Allow it to cool completely before cutting. Keep refrigerated.
